ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic hamburger entry (FOODON_00002383). No exact entry exists for a double cheeseburger with mushroom cream sauce served as a meal set. Closest parent match used; meal components (fries, beverage) are not individually coded here.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC covers hamburgers as a category. No specific code exists for a double cheeseburger mushroom meal set. Closest parent match applied at the burger category level.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No defensible SNOMED CT code identified for this specific fast food meal product. Left null per ontology honesty policy.

preparation_method: assembled with creamy mushroom sauce and processed cheese slices
allergens: soy (possible in sauce or patty seasoning)

serving.description: 1 upsized meal (double cheeseburger + large fries + Coke Zero)
serving.natural_language_portion: One upsized meal consisting of one Creamy Mushroom Double Cheeseburger (approximately 220 g), one large portion of french fries (approximately 154 g), and one Coke Zero beverage (approximately 246 g / 250 mL cup with ice), totalling approximately 620 g.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Cheeseburger, double patty, with condiments and vegetables (SR Legacy 21107); McDonald's French Fries Large (USDA Branded Foods); Coca-Cola Zero Sugar (USDA Branded Foods)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact HPB Singapore or USDA entry exists for the McDonald's Singapore Creamy Mushroom Double Cheeseburger Upsized Meal as a composite. Nutrient values are estimated by component-level adaptation: (1) double cheeseburger with condiments from USDA SR Legacy item 21107 (cheeseburger, double patty, with condiments and vegetables), adjusted for creamy mushroom sauce addition; (2) large french fries from USDA Branded Foods McDonald's French Fries entry; (3) Coke Zero contributes negligible macros. Per-100g values are derived by weighted composite of the burger (~220g) and fries (~154g) components, excluding the near-zero-calorie beverage, then normalised to 100g. Coke Zero macros are confirmed zero by formulation. Confidence is medium due to composite estimation.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; and University of Sydney GI Database — fast food burger and french fries analogous entries
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for the McDonald's Singapore Creamy Mushroom Double Cheeseburger Upsized Meal. GI estimated as 66 (medium) based on analogous fast food burger meal entries in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281-2283): white burger bun GI ~70, beef patty GI ~0 (protein/fat dominant), french fries GI ~63 (University of Sydney entry for McDonald's fries). Composite meal GI estimated at approximately 66 reflecting the mixed macronutrient profile. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (66 × 76.2) / 100 ≈ 50.3 g, where carbohydrate per serving = 28.5 g/100g × 374g solid food components ≈ 76.2 g net carbohydrate (excluding beverage).

hypertension.key_factor: Very high sodium content (~480 mg per 100g; ~2,976 mg per full meal serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this meal, as a single serving may provide approximately 125–150% of the recommended daily sodium intake of 2,000 mg, significantly elevating blood pressure risk.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load (~50 GL units per serving) and high refined carbohydrate content from white bun and french fries
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this meal or consume it only occasionally with careful carbohydrate budgeting, as the high glycemic load from the white bun and large fries can cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated saturated fat (~4.1 g/100g), trans fat (~0.3 g/100g), cholesterol (~42 mg/100g), and very high sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid regular consumption of this meal due to its combination of saturated fat, trans fat, and high sodium, all of which are independently associated with increased cardiovascular risk and adverse lipid profiles.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Creamy Mushroom Double Cheeseburger Upsized Meal is a limited-time McDonald's Singapore fast food meal set consisting of three components: (1) a Creamy Mushroom Double Cheeseburger — two grilled beef patties stacked on a toasted sesame-seed bun, topped with two slices of processed cheddar cheese, a proprietary creamy mushroom sauce, lettuce, pickles, mustard, and ketchup; (2) a large portion of McDonald's golden deep-fried french fries seasoned with salt; and (3) a Coke Zero (sugar-free cola beverage). The burger delivers a rich, savoury, umami-forward flavour profile from the mushroom cream sauce combined with the juicy beef patties and melted cheese, contrasted by the crispy, salty fries. The meal is ultra-processed and calorie-dense, providing approximately 1,538 kcal per full meal serving. It is certified Halal by MUIS (Majlis Ugama Islam Singapura) and is available at McDonald's Singapore outlets. Nutritionally, the meal is high in sodium (approximately 2,976 mg per serving), saturated fat, and refined carbohydrates, making it a high-concern food for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic load per serving is approximately 50 GL units, driven primarily by the white bun and large fries. The meal is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those on low-sodium or low-carbohydrate diets. It contains allergens including gluten, milk, and potentially soy and sesame.

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central for the McDonald's Singapore Creamy Mushroom Double Cheeseburger Upsized Meal as a composite product. Nutrient values are derived by component-level adaptation from USDA SR Legacy (double cheeseburger with condiments, item 21107) and USDA Branded Foods (McDonald's large fries), normalised to a per-100g composite basis excluding the near-zero-calorie Coke Zero. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database entries for burger meals and McDonald's fries. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. Food group corrected from input value 'Vegetables' to 'Mixed', as this is a composite fast food meal set containing beef, cheese, bread, and fries. 2. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this food category. 3. Vitamin E is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this composite meal. 4. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this food category. 5. Per-100g values represent the weighted composite of the burger (~220g) and fries (~154g) solid components only; Coke Zero is excluded from the per-100g calculation as it contributes negligible macronutrients (confirmed zero by product formulation). 6. Trans fat value of 0.3 g/100g is an estimate based on USDA SR Legacy data for double cheeseburger with condiments; actual value may vary by McDonald's Singapore formulation. 7. Serving size of 620g includes the Coke Zero beverage weight (~246g); solid food components total approximately 374g. 8. This is a limited-time McDonald's Singapore menu item; nutritional values may change with recipe reformulation. 9. Halal certification is based on McDonald's Singapore MUIS Halal certification as stated in cuisine_tags from source data.

improvement_actions_taken: Corrected food_group from 'Vegetables' (input error) to 'Mixed' reflecting composite meal nature.
Added full multilingual local_names for Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi per Singaporean cuisine G6 requirement.
Constructed per-100g nutrient values via component-level adaptation from USDA SR Legacy and USDA Branded Foods, as no exact source entry exists.
Documented all -1 sentinel values (vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, selenium_mcg) with explicit notes per G3 sentinel documentation rule.
Estimated GI as 66 (medium) from University of Sydney GI Database analogous entries; fully documented estimation basis.
Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases list for perfect consistency per Q8 aliases consistency rule, adding the missing romanized Chinese alias to the flat 'aliases' list.
Corrected ontology.foodon_match_type and ontology.foodex2_match_type from 'close' to 'closest_parent' to align with G5 ontology honesty rules.
Added zero-macro justification note for Coke Zero component confirming confirmed zero macros by product formulation.
